purpose: Decision draft after local OpenStack re-anchor microprobes. This is not a formal benchmark and is still blocked from paid API use.

why: Manual re-anchoring materially reduced alert-only shortcut solvability.
The instance-state cases now look much healthier.
The image-chain slice is still taxonomy-unstable.
One more hard but support-complete OpenStack case is still needed before the local gate looks credible.

approved_for_next_local_draft.alert_match: Took 20.03 seconds to spawn the instance on the hypervisor.
approved_for_next_local_draft.reason: open_alert_only failed at both noise levels while open_alert_context stayed correct at both noise levels.

approved_for_next_local_draft.alert_match: Took 20.29 seconds to spawn the instance on the hypervisor.
approved_for_next_local_draft.reason: Same healthy pattern as openstack_49 after replacing the direct spawned-success line.
This variant kept open_alert_only at zero while preserving correct open_alert_context at both noise levels.

review_only_candidates.reason: This is harder than the current in-use line, but the model still drifts toward OPENSTACK_CLEAN_STALE_BASE_CACHE_AND_RETRY under noise.

review_only_candidates.alert_match: Attempting claim: memory 2048 MB, disk 20 GB, vcpus 1 CPU
review_only_candidates.reason: No convincing weaker same-incident anchor has been identified yet.

drop_or_hold.reason: Too weak. Both open_alert_only and open_alert_context collapsed.
Too direct. open_alert_only succeeded at both noise levels.
Still pushes the model toward base-cache cleanup rather than repair-base-image.

needs: At least one additional hard OpenStack case with low alert-only solvability and stable action semantics.
Prefer a host-compatibility or inventory case only if it can be re-anchored without direct claim or explicit inventory-mismatch phrasing.
